reviews.review_text: This game wasn't the greatest when it launched, but modders did their best to make it a better experience. Eventually they made it a decent game through their work. Bethesda, egotistical children that they are, couldn't stand that somebody made their game better than them and released a patch that made the game worse and nuked the mods. Two years later, they did the same thing again just to make sure they got rid of anybody who still had the will to dis-enshittify their product. As always, Bethesda takes every chance they get to cut of their nose in order to spite their face.

It's a fairly good game except the developers can't stop messing with ♥♥♥♥ and keep ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ up everyone's mod lists. I can't play it right now because of the update they just did and won't be able to until certain mods get updated. Was this update necessary? no it was not. This is a pattern with these developers and it's getting really annoying. I've half a mind to uninstall this game simply because I'm tired of this pattern.

I would rather ♥♥♥♥ in my hands and clap than play this ♥♥♥♥ anymore. They only update this game to ragebait mod players and suck the wrinkly ♥♥♥♥♥ of people who pay for creation club.

This game is ELEVEN YEARS OLD, stop pissing on it with your useless updates.
Amazing open-world RPG with lots of exploration, fun combat, and great mods. Still worth playing today.
Stop updating the game, my mods do better than the original game

Stop updating the game and breaking everyone's mods. Bethesda loves to do it, going around being a bully on the playground knocking down everyone's sandcastles. Very annoying since literally the only thing that the update did was break mods.
Stop updating this ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ game, stop with the creation club ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥. Every time this game updates it ruins everything and adds nothing. No one wants these stupid updates, stop doing them!

I've just completed my 6th playtrhough (2nd on PC) and I'm still experiencing new quest lines for the first time. I know a lot of people love to hate on this game but it is and will be for the forseeable future; my favourite game ever.

I'd love to recommend this Bethesda based Fallout world, but I literally have to slow my computer down for this to play properly. It apparently only takes 20 seconds, but as they, the creators, have not done anything to integrate these changes within their own game, that also has a plethora of mods and continues to provide nothing, deserves nothing.

-be bethesda
-make a decent games that with mods can be good
-breaks game routinely with useless updates
-win?
would be great if Bethesda took their heads out of their asses and stopped updating the game
Another mediocre Bethesta game saved by modders, and all they do is constantly break mods with pointless updates.
The game is OLD!!! stop updating and ruining the mods that make the game still playable!!!
太他妈的离谱了，一更新MOD全都死亡。天天更新一群有的没有的功能。故意恶心人玩  This is freaking absurd! Every single update bricks all my mods. They just pile on random pointless features nonstop — they’re clearly trolling us.

Overhated game by Fatout fans, it is quite fun, mainly on survival mode. You should get some community fixes though because despite having been updated many times in the almost 11 years its been out, there are still a variety of bugs that will detract from your experience. The funny glitches with ragdoll physics and whatnot will not be affected and imo, are a highlight of playing this game. Game also has some issues running in borderless windowed, so I play in fullscreen but have to alt tab when loading in or out of interiors as the load times will be crazy long if I don't.

I love & am hopelessly addicted to this game (including all the DLCs which I actually do really like). What makes the game for me though is the mods. I absolutely love the cc ghoulification mod but also the extra settlment and companion management ones from nexusmods. There's just so much story to be added and endless adjustment. Much fun

I absolutely love this game, don't get me wrong, but the latest updates have made this game buggier than ever. If the game wasn't unplayable without mods for you before, it likely will be now (as is my case). That wouldn't be a problem on its own, with such a large modding community for this game, except that every update will break all your mods. Unless you are willing to go to further lengths than should be necessary to stop your game from updating entirely, then consider this game to be unplayable due to bugs.

 So many quests, including the main questline, can easily be unintentionally bugged and broken entirely if you don't do things in the exact correct order, or because the dialogue is bugged, or just because the game itself isn't stable.

I really love the Fallout series, but the latest updates have really ruined the experience to the point that I'd almost rather drop the game entirely rather than go to all the effort needed to make it work again. It's a shame, really.

You arrogant, egotistical dumbass mother ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, QUIT UPDATING THIS ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ GAME! The game's modding scene gets ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ broken every single update they release, ON A NEARLY 11 YEAR OLD GAME! Ever since your retarded TV show first aired, you've broken this game time and again year over year with your so-called "updates."
The only good thing about this game is the mods that make it playable and each update not only makes the mods not work but only adds more bugs.

Amazing game, ♥♥♥♥ company.
Their goal in life is just to break mods with useless updates that no one asked for.

Bethesda has once again fumbled the bag that is Fallout 4 by sending out yet another update that makes the game objectively worse in an attempt to make even more money. 
Todd is a spiteful old ♥♥♥♥ who's upset that we don't need his ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ divine intervention to make this game good, so he 'updates' it every year or so to make everything in it noticeably worse than it was before. This game is going to turn 12 this year, and Bethesda can't just leave the entirely offline, singleplayer game to its devices because they want even more money in their grubby little sausage fingers.
Don't even bother buying this. They will publish totally unnecessary updaters once in a while that brick every mod - game without mods i just utter garbage. FU

Game is Broken as ♥♥♥♥ now. In 20 hours I never got past the starting missions because their are so many game bugs. 2 of them game breaking. First I couldn't get out the starting pod for 2 hours I was frozen inside and had to restart from scratch. Then i wasted hour trying to fix the pip-boy color but its busted blacked and white. Couldn't fix it ignore it but then I spent 3 hours trying to fix my pip-boy because every time I got out my power armor it went invisible and you cant see the map or items again. 

Even using AI to help me I cant get this crap to just work normal, and i got no mods on other then what the came came with. WTF DID THEY GIVE YOU MODS THAT BREAK THE ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ GAME! As a old school fan to every one, do not give Bethesda any more money. They are ♥♥♥♥ now and every thing they touch, even a update to a old game is garbage and broken. I give up on them.
Unneccessary updates ruin mods. Stop breaking a great game

decided to play again just to see the game receive another useless update just to ruin mods once more 
♥♥♥♥ bethesda and ♥♥♥♥ todd howard. may you suffer everlasting pain in hell for eternity and evermore
the most generous review this garbage deserves
Everytime they update the game to sell the lamest cosmetics imaginable, they FCKD my game, stop UPDATING THE GAMEEEEE JUST LET US MOD IN PEACE.
♥♥♥♥ you Todd for your ♥♥♥♥♥♥ update that did ♥♥♥♥ all but make modding my game the biggest headache imaginable
Deadass had to start my entire save file over because CC borked my og one, still 100%ed the game tho, no I wont recommened this seaming pile of ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥

The Fallout franchise by Bethesda ain't known for their story as it's kinda boring so don't expect to be thrilled with the main quest here, the player's decisions in game feels ultimately irrelevant in the end and the characters are so lame that it's clear not much thought were given to their backstories (I'm looking at you main antagonist). The presentation of the in universe lore is inconsistent at best, the factions felt underwhelming and the overall game screams wasted potential. If you want to play an RPG with an actual story and the inclusion of player choices that matters? Play Baldurs Gate or Mass Effect instead.

So despite it's dogwater story among many of it's flaws, why is Fallout 4 one of my favorite games?

It is is a Psuedo-Sandbox game that is built on top of an interesting universe with tons of customizations and an active modding community!

The game feels liberating when you're not restricted to the main story. You are given the choice to do a side quests in your own way, discover new locations by exploring on your own, customize your weapons and built your settlement with some degree of freedom. Interactable Items and scraps scattered around the wasteland have a purpose and when you pick up that rusty can, your monkey brain just feels pure satisfaction. Also the gore system feels satisfying like blowing a raider's brains out with a shotgun using VATS.

If you just play the game as how it is, overlook many of it's flaws and don't take the story seriously, it's damn enjoyable. Now add on top of that with mods and it's guranteed you'll be having the time of your life! Bethesda should thank the Modding community for carrying this franchise!

warning do not buy fallout 4 through steam next gen update has broken the game beyond repair black screens on startup audio out of sync got to cap the game to 60fps in order to keep it stable so bad how aaa devs bring games like this out while its broken not at all impressed ran better on console cant run the game on ultra without it crashing my specs are intel core ultra i9 24 cores 5090gpu 200gb of ddr5 ram 2tb ssd so its defo not my rig im yet to try it through xbox but i really dont hold much hope such a shame even looked up fixes on google and youtube nothing worked...

This was the first Fallout game I played and after finishing every other Fallout game, this is my favourite of them all.
I recommend every Fallout title as each one has its ups and downs, but I would specially recommend this one if you like settlement building mechanics and tinkering with your gear.

I really enjoy the Fallout games. They give you so much to explore, and there are always unique things to find in the world by just wandering. Building settlements was definitely different coming from the third game, but it was a neat addition. Whether you are new to Fallout or have played it before, you should definitely pick this game up and try it out. Also, I highly recommend checking out the mods for the game since there is so much you can do in that direction as well.

Thanks for the garbage slop update Bugthesda! I sure do love having my save completely bricked because I'm forced to update this single player game that's treated like live service, BREAKING EVERY MOD IN THE PROCESS. Y'all can absolutely gfys lol

I've played this game extensively. Bethesda can go ♥♥♥♥ themselves with a cactus for continuously messing with this game for the purpose of pushing Creation Club and screwing over the modders who make their game what it should be after they fob us off with their lazy programming, poor writing, and overall refusal to evolve as a company. This was my first Fallout game, so it has nostalgia for me, but if it wasn't for that I honestly don't know if I would have even played this game the whole way through if I came to it now for the first time. Unless the next Fallout gets insanely good reviews, I won't be buying it purely because I have ZERO faith in Bethesda. They don't care about their player base,  they're arrogant and refuse to listen to criticism, and they seem to think they're entitled to accolades they haven't even earned. The reason I have so many hours in FO4 isn't because of Bethesda - it's because of the modders. If it wasn't for them, I probably would have put this game down a long time ago and never bothered with it again. So go to Hell, Bethesda. No matter how many times you patch this game to screw us, you'll never erase the reality of your subpar performance.

While the Video Game is good, be it combat, narrative or otherwise, the constant unneeded updates as of late, most likely a corporate attempt at higher profits, are preventing me from enjoying my experience and are a serious turn off, specialy since this wasn't supposed to be a live service game.

My main reason for not recommending is because bethesda doesn't understand their player community at all or what they want. Its been years since any kind of update has been wanted for FO4, and they still routinely update random bs regarding creation club or console support. It constantly breaks mods, makes it difficult for modders and the community to interact with the game, and only shows how greedy bethesda is. The game itself is decent, but its more a fallout flavored apocalyptic game than a real fallout game, that's my opinion, ymmv.
the game is great. but they keep removing cheats hacks and mods they cant charge you for. Wankers

I can't believe I am saying this but stop updating this game. You left it alone for years and now you come back adding useless ♥♥♥♥♥ and unironically more bugs. The worst of it is now many mods don't work and now mods have to divide themselves between your pre update and post updates. You are a failure of a company that have to destroy what your community create just to squeeze that little money out of the game that you made a decade ago because all of your new games are all failures.

★★★★☆

my partner introduced me to the fallout universe and I fell absolutely in love with it.

I'm a very, very, very casual gamer and have noticed that the very easy difficulty is still quite hard. while most people enjoy a challenge; I enjoy the story and pretty much no challenge.
this was hard to achieve in the game and I had to resort to mods to make my game easier.

with that being said: I am so proud of myself for finishing this game once and I am looking forward to discovering the other endings and side missions in this world at some other point.

because the game did not feel very easy to me even though I wanted to play on very easy I will give it 4/5.
it very well deserves the 4 other stars for the wonderful writing and voice acting, I loved nearly every second of this game. and I barely finish a story game so it tells me personally a lot that I managed to finish the (main)storyline.

if you ever question to play the game: yes! play it! it's amazing!
meanwhile I am working on my new vegas playthrough as well and that is so far also a very big recommend!

I started Fallout 4 expecting a smooth experience, especially since my PC is more than capable of running a game from 2015. Unfortunately, the game has been a technical mess from the start.

Pros:

1. When the game actually works, I can run it at maximum settings with excellent performance and high FPS.

Cons:

1. Frequent crashes to desktop.
2. Severe audio synchronization issues that make conversations and gameplay feel broken.
3. Audio randomly cuts out completely, requiring a full system reboot to restore sound.
4. Spent more time troubleshooting than actually playing.

It's frustrating that a game this old still suffers from such major technical problems. The few moments where everything works show the potential of the game, but constant crashes and audio issues make it difficult to recommend. A game shouldn't require this much effort just to function properly.

The fact Bethesda now expects us to pay money for mods on a game from 2015 is laughable and absurd. I have 1200+ hours of modded play time in this game and I will not be playing anymore. Seriously unbelievable. How about instead of adding microtransactions to an 11-year-old game, you guys work on creating and putting out something new?
offensively bad when you consider its part of the fallout series. the only way it is playable is if you mod to the point of being just a worse stalker and at that point just play stalker.

This is my favorite Fallout game. Lots of content and pretty interesting storyline with multiple endings. Fallout has a very interesting game world and I would recommend it to anyone who likes to play action RPG games.
game is unplayable not a single main or side questline is possible without softlocks.

The story and the graphics are great, but ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ THE WEAPON VARIETY SUCKS CXCKKKK!!!!!

I could not stand playing this game in vanilla, mods are a must! Luckily, I already played this game several times over with mods before they killed off F4SE. It was great, multiple companions, sweet weapon loadouts, it was bliss.

But now...it sucks.

TLDR: play this game if you don't care much about story, like to mod video games - even just so they run properly.

why this game annoys the ♥♥♥♥ out of me.
The world is the almost singlehandedly weakest part of this ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ game if you start thinking about the characters or the location for even a few minutes. New Vegas had a good reason as to why there was very few people. water. In fallout 4 there is no explanation as to why there isn't, after 210 years, a bigger city than a football stadium. one of the biggest aspects about humanity is that we're literally the cockroaches of mammals - because of technology. how has humanity not advanced further than a stadium sized city in one of the easiest places to survive in America without technology?
The characters ARE the worst part of this world. why does a literal robot, codsworth, that you literally bought give two ♥♥♥♥♥ about who you side with and what you do? does that mean he has free will? does that mean when you bought him pre-war you bought a robo-slave? this isn't explored in any meaningful way. why doesn't piper wright, an investigative journalist want access to secretive factions like the institute or brotherhood of steel? in the real world investigative journalists literally talk to cartel drug runners for a short interview, why doesn't piper abandon her morals for a literal journalism gold mine? why the hell does Paladin Danse expect a random person to help him with no promise of pay to fight synths - the literal boogeyman of the commonwealth?
The factions, like the characters, are just as braindead. I have very little experience with Fallout4 factions, but from the little I've seen, I am disappointed. why does the brotherhood of steel, a large, high tech faction not have the resources to manufacture ammunition and weapons for the soldiers to the point where their soldiers have to PAY for their bullets? yet the brotherhood of steel somehow has the infrastructure to sustain a giant blimp, multiple vertibirds - military helicopters, and likely countless suits of power armor that requires constant powering through fusion cells. There is every reason for brotherhood soldiers to extort settlements, yet they barely show that. and why hasn't the brotherhood worked around this issue, like taking on defense contracts for towns so they can actually pay for their soldier's ammo. and on payment, why is every faction allergic to paying you decently for doing something for them?
secondly, the choices for who gets to rule the commonwealth have been boiled down into simplistic moral choices, do you want bad faction to rule wasteland or good faction to rule wasteland. The institute - a faction that creates synths to commit warcrimes, kidnaps children and replaces people - the obviously bad option. the minutemen - protects settlements. to compare, lets look at fallout new Vegas's options for who gets to rule hoover dam. Mr. House - an autocrat with 1st class citizens who got there by being wealthier than the rest. he does little to support the few people outside the strip yet the people who live inside the strip undoubtedly live far better lives than any other faction, there's little to no corruption. he runs his government like a business, yet no one can say he runs his business poorly. he united and reformed multiple savage tribes inside Vegas into more civilized tribes that run casinos. a benevolent autocrat to those with deep enough pockets and an uncaring father to those who can't pay. Either Fallout 4 gives you simplistic options because they think you're too stupid to understand complexity or they think you don't care.
thirdly, the missions in fallout new Vegas are better too. take the mission 'Crazy, Crazy, Crazy'. Where Tabitha a supermutant cult leader had kidnapped a ghoul mechanic to repair her best friend, a Mr. Handy robot. and said ghoul is calling for help on the radio Tabitha is using to communicate to her people. functionally she's just an excuse as to why a companion is there. yet I still remember this quest long after playing new Vegas, however I've already forgotten much of the slop present in this game.
on design, this game is undoubtedly ugly for the time period and for the triple a devs. The graphics are muddy for the time. multiple guns look like they were modeled by people who haven't even shot a gun - multiple guns would spit shells into your face if they were actually fired how Nate/Nancy fired them.
Anyway, to sum up a long review. this game thinks you're too stupid to comprehend complex choices. and it sanitizes a world that shouldn't be sanitized. when you ask to get paid for difficult work the world calls you greedy. this is a game is a 5 that people say is an 8. if you want a good, somewhat modern fallout game, play Fallout New Vegas.
Why does bethesda keep updating a 10 year old game when it adds literally nothing and just breaks all the mods
game has been broken for years. why wont they fix it. crashes within a hour of starting each time and causes me to have to restart my pc everytime.

It comes across as foolish when one attempts to monetize community-made mods.
Then, you subsequently release an 'anniversary edition' in multiple games that reinforces your decision. Mind you, years and years after it was made known a problem. 

Somehow it's easy to see Bethesda products as modern day flops when they failed only after succeeding in doing the same thing over and over again. Instead of doing the same thing again, you harvest your fans' passions for the ART to supplement your own. [wallet] 

It makes me sad to see something that use to matter so deeply to me be corrupted by the concept of money over art.

First things first. It's now 2026 and everyone knows what Fallout 4 is and what it isn't, and how it's not [i]really[/i] a Fallout game and arguably not even a Role Playing Game to begin with. But we don't care, because the game is a lot of fun anyways and if you haven't played it I recommend you do so, unless you are a hardcore fan of the original Fallout who cannot stand to see how Bethesda has changed the franchise into something completely different, but you know that already. For me the game provides potentially endless hours of fun exploration, combat, crafting, leveling and much more, and it can all be enhanced and customized to your liking with the help of mods made by the amazing modding community that this game is fortunate to have and that it doesn't deserve. That's good enough for me, it gets a recommendation on that alone.

Now, despite having a few hundred hours of fun with this game and recommending you to play it, I have to leave a negative review here on Steam for a particular reason: Forced updates. This is the bane of people who like to play the game with a modded setup (a [i]lot[/i] of people, myself included), because updates often break mod compatibility, so you can say bye bye to your modded playthrough for a while every time Bethesda decides to push an update for no good reason to what is now an 11 year old game. Honestly they should be all hands on deck working on The Elder Scrolls VI instead of wasting their man hours updating this. There are ways to [i]try[/i] and circumvent these updates (which often doesn't last long) and to downgrade the game after it forcibly updates without your permission yet again, but it's a pain in the rear that paying customers should not have to suffer through and it's honestly disrespectful to the fanbase here on Steam.

For this reason I recommend that you don't buy the game on Steam, and that you acquire it through other means instead. I hear that the version you can buy on GOG is only patched up to 1.10.163 which is an old version of the game that is the best for mod compatibility, and it's the one that I always downgrade to after every forced update. I know we all love how convenient Steam is and we already have the majority of our games here, but it's not worth it for Fallout 4, just get it somewhere else instead and save yourself the pain of having to downgrade the game every 6 months or so. I would buy it on GOG but I don't feel like paying for a game I already paid for [i]again[/i] just to avoid Bethesda's malicious updates. I shouldn't have to pay twice just to avoid mod breaking updates.

All that said, I will edit this review from a negative rating to a positive one [i]only[/i] if/when Bethesda does the following: Simply add a new branch for the 1.10.163 version of the game. Just let us opt into a different version through the "Game Versions & Betas" section on the Properties of the game, a branch that stays on 1.10.163 and [i]never[/i] gets [i]any[/i] updates after that. That's it, that's all they need to do to make me happy, and it's something many devs do already, RESIDENT EVIL 2 Remake and Gothic I and II are good examples of games with multiple branches. But it's 2026 and Bethesda still hasn't done it, because they don't care about their players on Steam, so I'm not holding my breath. Don't buy Fallout 4 on Steam, get it somewhere else.

I'm not sure how to rate this game. It is a massive and fun open world game, but the vanilla version has major detracting factors.

My main issue is you can do nearly everything on a single playthrough. And to be fair, the first playthrough is great, you feel the "living" world. Then the second playthrough you realize choices mean nothing and your decisions don't affect the story or world. Specific things may change, but the world does not respond. 

So you download some great community mods that enhance and expand the world in wonderful ways! Choices matter, new branches in the main quest, a more living world. Then Bethesda updates. Updates the decade old game so they can add their own paid mods. And the game breaks. And community mods break. (Luckily the community modders had a solution, a mod to revert the game to an older version.) 

Bethesda, please stop updating the game. Just leave the game alone, go distract yourself with Starfield 2. Thx.

Stars:
Vanilla Game (First Playthrough): 6/10 (8/10).
Add Community Mods: 9/10.
Current Update Cycle: 3/10.
The newest update broke all my mods, so now my only option is to play the bug-ridden, unstable, and unfun vanilla version of the game. I'm not going to do that.

Game is super broken yet features intense vast amounts of ingame microtransactions

Game features a "survival mode" where you get no savepoints, except for sleeping. Yet the game is so broken, that you will keep losing your progress to basic things that just break the game to the point where the game can even freeze up your entire operating system.

Terrible unfinished product, Bethesda doesnt give a ♥♥♥♥

For a game released in 2015 it's a masterpiece incredible open world great combat impelling story. It can be buggy at times and I wish updates wouldn't break mods but other than that it's a great game would definitely recommend picking it up even today.
Good game, also because it introduced me to a lot of old timey bangers

i downgraded my game so i could keep playing the mods i had but if they would work at it and do a better job with the updates so it doesnt effect mods  but i get it the code is different but why add creation crap just let people buy it if they want to play that stuff dont force it down our throats
Great game, one of the best rpgs ever. However, there's a good fraction of bugs.

Fallout 4 is a bad game, with terrible shooting mechanics, bad AI, unresponsive hit reactions, and overinflated health pools, while also boasting an oversimplistic progression system with lame percentage-based upgrades. The weapon upgrade system features the same issues. Instead of meaningfully altering gameplay, upgrades serve as flat damage increases or somehow decrease damage when turning a semi-automatic weapon fully automatic. Instead of balancing these systems with a more robust ammo economy and/or harsher recoil penalties, they simply make an automatic weapon do 20% of the damage of a semi-automatic one. Healing items are so plentiful that it's damn near impossible to run out of them even on the hardest difficulty. On top of that, instead of limiting how many items the player can use, it's possible to pause the game, use 200 healing items and continue shooting as if nothing happened. 
On a positive note, the map is vast and filled with interesting locations that make absolutely zero sense, but in a way it’s fulfilling to see different things. Unfortunately, everything surrounding this exploration is dysfunctional.
This game features a terrible, downright nonsensical story following the quest of the world’s worst absent father or mother searching for their son. As it is necessary to point out, the male protagonist is a notorious war criminal known as Nate the Rake (according to the lead writer, and yes, I’m serious, look it up). Within a week of witnessing your wife’s brutal assassination by a Corn Flakes brand mascot (this is also not a joke, he is named Kellogg for some unknown reason), you proceed to build an entire civilization’s worth of outposts and find a replacement partner as if nothing happened and your son hadn’t been kidnapped. Oh, the wonders of a poorly written RPG narrative that fails in almost every conceivable way to account for player choice.
Fallout 4 had a pretty robust modding community that made replaying the game cool, interesting, and customizable. Then, after six years of not touching the game, they randomly decided to launch a billion broken updates that split mods across different game versions and made modding the game a pain in the ass. All of this because they wanted to sell ♥♥♥♥♥♥ paid mods. Yipeeee. Idk, 3.5/10.

Laggy mess even with "fix" mods, never works how its supposed to. DO NOT BUY. if u want to play a fallout... PLAY FALLOUT NV; the only one not made by bethesdanyahu. ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ dev team ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ company simple as that

The Constant updates by Bethesda are absurd. You know the only reason people play your games for as long as they do is because of the modding community. We don't want your paid mods ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ that already bricked our consoles. And what's worse is Steam has the ability for you to keep older versions of the game to roll them back but this is not active for your titles. Your business practices are getting ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ and I genuinely hope the worst for your current management.

If you like modding the game to be something more intresting then base Bethesda 'Accept a quest' gameplay, remember Bethesda knows modders keep the 10 year old game alive.... but for some reason they love updating and breaking mods.

It's a great shooter and a terrible RPG. The writing is meh in a lot of places, but let's be real, we didn't come here for the writing. The combat is satisfying. In-depth weapon modification system allows enough customization to support any playstyle. Its open world allows excellent replayability. Plethora of community-made mods keep this game alive many ears after launch. All in all, it's worth your money

Omg can you guys please just stop updating the freaking game and focus on a new one? You break all of the updates made by fans that are FAR better than any of the updates you've made since Far Harbor (which I had JUST got to in my last playthrough)

It's the only great game that I wish the developer would STOP updating. the modders and modding scene is absolutely great, and 80-90% of the time they manage to fix problems from the base game, but there isn't an option to roll back to a previous version of the game that was more stable and everyone's been working with for years, so, uh. 

It's kinda like trying to patch holes in the boat while there's another guy trying to stitch on half-built pieces to the hull, that you aren't allowed to take out. 

In conclusion, I'd recommend the mods, not Todd get on steep discount if you can.

I'm just echoing every other opinion at this point. honestly if i hadn't grown up with these games i think i would hate them. the writing is horribly shallow and the gameplay of a beloved RPG franchise has been reduced into a sloppy looter shooter like you'd get out of a gas station slushy machine. honestly the second part would be forgivable if the game had more than a handful of passable stories to tell. i love this game, but every year bethesda proves they dont deserve our money more and more.

I will never not love this game. It's by no means the best in the series. That goes to both New Vegas and Fallout 2 for me, but it has my top 2 favorite characters in the entire franchise, show included. I can't praise Nick Valentine and Hancock enough. They're bar none the most charismatic and interesting characters, even if they don't go nearly in depth enough with Hancock's past and connection to Mayor McDonough. There's still so much to love. The combat is fun, and the story, though a bit limiting for an RPG is still worth the time and effort. Honestly, the main issue I have is in how it forces you into a pre-established relationship and family background, when Bethesda is usually so good at avoiding that, even with James in Fallout 3. There's WAY more wiggle-room for how you handle that story as opposed to Nate or Nora.

Fallout 4 is one of those games you keep coming back to. The main story is kinda bland, but the side quests and random wasteland encounters make it worth replaying. What really keeps it alive is the modding scene which can keep the endless combat encounters fresh af, while environment is diverse enough make the Commonwealth feel replayable. The legendary enemy system is a neat touch too, keeps combat spicy even after hundreds of hours.

Bugs, bugs, and more bugs.

Some of these bugs are game-breaking and force you to reload from a previous save. There are even bugs that require you to use the command console or go online to find workarounds just to advance the story.

You would think a game that has been out for over a decade would have these bugs patched by now. But no, they are still there. At this point, you basically need to download mods just to fix problems the developer should have fixed themselves.

Fallout 4 is the kind of game you boot up "just for an hour" and suddenly it's 2am and you've rebuilt an entire settlement, stumbled into a raider camp you weren't supposed to clear for another ten levels, and somehow ended up in a submarine. It's messy, it's sprawling, and honestly — that's the appeal.

The Commonwealth is one of the most realized open worlds Bethesda has ever shipped. Boston's ruined skyline feels genuinely alive in a way the older Fallout games couldn't quite pull off, and the environmental storytelling — notes, skeletons, half-finished meals still on the table — does a quiet, brilliant job of making you feel like the apocalypse actually happened here. The main story surrounding your search for your missing son is emotionally grounded in a way that hooks you early, even if the later factions and their competing agendas get a little muddy.

 If you're coming from New Vegas expecting the same level of roleplay depth, temper expectations. The dialogue wheel strips out a lot of the creative freedom that made New Vegas feel like a proper RPG, and your character's choices rarely ripple outward in the same meaningful way. You're more "action hero with a backstory" than "person shaped entirely by your decisions."

That said — the jump in visual fidelity over its predecessors is hard to overstate. The lighting, the weather systems, the way the Charles River reflects a setting sun through the skeletal ruins of downtown Boston — it genuinely earns its price of admission on atmosphere alone. Combat is tighter, VATS is smoother, and the settlement building system (while completely optional) is a surprisingly deep rabbit hole if you let it pull you in.

But here's the real reason Fallout 4 has the legs it does: the modding community. Years after release, people are still uploading content daily — new quests, voiced companions, total overhauls, new weapons, new worldspaces, bug fixes Bethesda never got around to. If you're willing to spend an afternoon building a mod list, you're not just playing Fallout 4 anymore. You're playing a version of it that didn't exist when the game shipped. The community has essentially turned it into a living game, and that longevity is something very few titles ever achieve.

It's not New Vegas. It was never going to be. But as a first-person RPG with incredible atmosphere, a surprisingly emotional core, and one of the most active modding scenes in gaming — it absolutely earns a spot in your library.

A lot of people will tell you that “new Vegas is better” because it has a better story but they are LIARS. Fallout 4 is an amazing game with a great story and super fun and easy to learn gameplay. I also love the settlement building in this game, which is not in the other fallout games. I’m not saying that the other fallout games are bad, but for me and for other people looking to get into fallout, fallout 4 is the most modern fallout experience. My only issues with this game is that the game is very buggy and the graphics have not held up super well, but that can be fixed with mods. Also, the dlcs in this game are really great. Despite its problems, fallout 4 is one of the best games I’ve ever played. 10/10.
